ocr.text: The first table presents data on the formulation effects, with headers including Formulation, GI, % Change from white bread
control, and P Value. The Control group has a GI of 59.3 ± 24.7, with no change or P Value indicated. The 1500 mg Capsule
exhibits a GI of 61.9 ± 2.6, a % Change from white bread control of -4.39, and a P Value of 0.77. The 2000 mg Capsule shows a
GI of 45.1 ± 14.2, a % Change of 24.01, and a P Value of 0.076. The 3000 mg Capsule has a GI of 46.8 ± 12.5, with a %
Change of 21.05 and a P Value of 0.11. The 1500 mg Powder has a GI of 43.6 ± 15.7, a % Change of 26.41, and a P Value of
0.11. The 2000 mg Powder presents a GI of 45.2 ± 14.1, with a % Change of 23.76 and a P Value of 0.16. Finally, the 3000 mg
Powder has a GI of 39.1 ± 20.2, a % Change of 34.11, and a P Value of 0.023. The second table details regulators with headers
as Name of regulator, Peptide, Group, and Role of mechanism, where Rgg corresponds to SHP, ComS (XIP), classified under
Streptococci for Commensalism/Virulence; NprR associates with NprX in Bacilli for Necrotrophism; PlcR with PapR in Bacilli
for Virulence; PrgX, TraA with cCF10 iCF10 in Enterococci for Virulence; and TprA with PhrA in Streptococci for
Commensalism.
Formulation  GI  % Change from white bread control  P Value
Control  59.3 ± 24.7
Name of regulator  Peptide  Group  Role of mechanism
Rgg  SHP, ComS (XIP)  Streptococci  Commensalism/Virulence
NprR  NprX  Bacilli  Necrotrophism
PlcR  PapR  Bacilli  Virulence
PrgX, TraA  cCF10 iCF10  Enterococci  Virulence
TprA  PhrA  Streptococci  Commensalism
